summary: The Edge-statistics Conjecture was fully resolved within months of its arXiv posting. Kwan, Sudakov and Tran (arXiv:1807.05202) proved the conjecture for the range Ω(k) ≤ ℓ ≤ binom(k,2)/2, and Fox and Sauermann (arXiv:1809.01352, Advances in Combinatorics 2020:4) completed the proof by handling the remaining case 1 ≤ ℓ ≤ C·k; Martinsson, Mousset, Noever and Trujić independently covered the sublinear regime ℓ ≪ k^{6/5}. The hypergraph generalisation suggested in the original paper was subsequently proved by Jain, Kwan, Mubayi and Tran (arXiv:2505.03954, IMRN 2025).

since_posted.title: Anticoncentration for subgraph statistics
since_posted.authors: Matthew Kwan, Benny Sudakov, Tuan Tran
since_posted.claim: Proves the Edge-statistics Conjecture for the linear range Ω(k) ≤ ℓ ≤ binom(k,2)/2, establishing ind(k,ℓ) ≤ log^{O(1)}(ℓ*/k) · sqrt(k/ℓ*) where ℓ* = min{ℓ, binom(k,2) − ℓ}.

since_posted.title: A Completion of the Proof of the Edge-statistics Conjecture
since_posted.claim: Completes the full proof of the conjecture by handling the remaining case 1 ≤ ℓ ≤ C·k (Theorem 1.2), together with Kwan–Sudakov–Tran which covered C·k ≤ ℓ ≤ binom(k,2)/2, establishing ind(k,ℓ) ≤ 1/e + o_k(1) for all 0 < ℓ < binom(k,2).

since_posted.title: The edge-statistics conjecture for hypergraphs
since_posted.authors: Vishesh Jain, Matthew Kwan, Dhruv Mubayi, Tuan Tran
since_posted.claim: Proves the hypergraph generalisation of the conjecture suggested by Alon–Hefetz–Krivelevich–Tyomkyn: for r-uniform hypergraphs the fraction of k-vertex subsets spanning exactly ℓ edges is at most 1/e + ε for k sufficiently large whenever ℓ is neither 0 nor maximal.

internal_refs_verified.note: WebFetch confirms title 'A Completion of the Proof of the Edge-statistics Conjecture', authors Fox and Sauermann, and that it completes the proof by handling 1 ≤ ℓ ≤ C·k; published in Advances in Combinatorics 2020:4 as stated in the contribution text.

search_queries: Edge-statistics Conjecture ind(k,l) 1/e Kwan Sudakov Tran proof
Kwan Sudakov Tran "edge-statistics" arXiv ind(k,l) conjecture proof 2018 2019
notes: Martinsson, Mousset, Noever and Trujić (arXiv:1809.02576, Israel Journal of Mathematics) independently proved the conjecture for ℓ ≪ k^{6/5}; their paper appeared in search results but was not WebFetched, so it is noted here rather than included in since_posted. The Fox–Sauermann paper (1809.01352) is the canonical completion cited in the literature, with the Kwan–Sudakov–Tran paper covering the complementary linear range.
